steps.title: Identify what the blank refers to
steps.content: The antecedent is “Félix González-Torres” — one person, singular. The blank must be a pronoun that (1) agrees in number with that singular antecedent and (2) clearly indicates it was González-Torres himself who created the additional installations.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
Test each pronoun against the singular antecedent Félix González-Torres:
  • Choice A — ‘They all’: Plural — does not agree with the singular antecedent González-Torres, and does not clearly refer to him. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘Those’: Plural — does not agree with the singular antecedent, and does not clearly refer to González-Torres. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Both’: Implies exactly two entities — does not agree with the singular antecedent and introduces an ambiguity about what two things are meant. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘He’: Singular masculine pronoun — agrees with the singular antecedent González-Torres and unambiguously identifies him as the one who created the additional installations. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
